title: mappia racemosa var. typica
intro: Mappia racemosa var. typica, also known as the Siam root or Thai turmeric, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Mappia racemosa var. typica include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant is often prepared for herbal use by making a decoction with the roots, which is then consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
